reviews.review_msg: lectures are pretty much not worth going to because he just reads off the slides. he's quite old, so sometimes some things can be confusing when he's explaining. look over the slides and you'll be fine. he made the midterm into weekly quizzes so be sure to show up to tutorials! the course was interesting, but i wouldn't take it w this prof again

I just want to give him a hug. Laurent is SO sweet and very passionate about what he teaches. However, he is up there in age and is quite disorganized. He tries SO hard to engage the students even when they are dry. Expect LOADS of reading, and memorization of key concepts for the final and midterm. A prof with a LOT of heart!

He's a really sweet old professor who's passionate about what he taught. IMO ratings are skewed heavily by old reviews which don't reflect how he is today. His lecture slides are in-depth and posted after class but he adds additional info in lectures so it's worth going. Would give a 5 but the final's questions were not expected at all.

This class has been extremely challenging. The syllabus feels disorganized, and many of the questions provided are confusing or seem framed in a way that only the lecturer fully understands. The workload is heavy, requiring extensive reading.

Laurent's lecture may be heavy but his exams are also based on that. His slides are informative, which is helpful during exam periods. He is also caring and open to having a deeper discussion on the topic if you show up during office hours and express your interests.

Laurent is very passionate about the material he teaches but is terrible at teaching. He lectures as if everyone in the class has the same passion and level of comprehension of the material as he does. He does very little to structure his course in a way where you understand the concepts; instead, he only promotes memorization.

He was a super nice guy & was very clearly knowledgeable on the subject! However, his lectures were often scattered, and he tends to go off on tangents that aren't really applicable to the course. You can skip the lectures and just make sure to do the readings, as they have all the information needed for exams. Exams and papers were graded fairly!

The amount you have to read for this course is crazy. I feel bad for the prof because he's honestly quite old so I don't blame him, but he just reads off slides for every lecture. Lectures are extremely hard to sit through because his teaching style and because the content is really boring
very friendly and approachable. But the lectures are just him reading off the slides which makes it difficult to engage. Get ready to do a lot of reading
Nice guy, a fair marker if you put in the work.
